title: The King James Version of the Bible
description: The canonical King James English translation of the Bible, central to Christian scripture and influential in English-language religious literature.

title: The Koran
description: An English translation of the Qur'an, the central sacred text of Islam, presenting revelations associated with the Prophet Muhammad.

title: The Analects of Confucius
description: The foundational text of Confucianism, a record of the sayings of Confucius and his conversations with his disciples.

title: The Upanishads
description: Ancient Indian philosophical and religious texts that explore Brahman, Atman, liberation, and the foundations of Vedantic thought.

title: The Ramayan of Valmiki
description: An English verse translation of the Ramayana, one of India's great mythological epics, narrating the deeds of Rama, Sita, Hanuman, and Ravana.

title: The Dhammapada
description: A celebrated collection of sayings attributed to the Buddha, emphasizing ethics, mindfulness, wisdom, and the path to liberation.

title: The Tao Teh King
description: James Legge's English translation of the Tao Te Ching, a foundational Daoist text on the Way, virtue, simplicity, and non-action.

title: Myths and Legends of China
description: A broad collection of Chinese myths, legends, deities, culture heroes, creation stories, and folk religious traditions.

title: Buddhist Psalms
description: English translations of devotional Japanese Buddhist poems associated with Shinran Shonin and Pure Land religious thought.

title: The Book of the Dead
description: An English rendering of ancient Egyptian funerary texts intended to guide the deceased through judgment and the afterlife.

title: The Babylonian Legends of the Creation
description: A translation and discussion of Babylonian creation mythology, including themes of cosmic order, divine conflict, and the origins of the world.

title: The Iliad
description: An ancient Greek epic poem attributed to Homer, set during the Trojan War and focusing on the quarrel between King Agamemnon and the warrior Achilles.

title: The Poetic Edda
description: A collection of Old Norse mythological and heroic poems, preserving major stories about Odin, Thor, Loki, Ragnarok, and legendary heroes.

title: Kalevala The Epic Poem of Finland
description: The Finnish national epic, compiled from oral poetry, featuring creation myths, magic songs, heroes, shamans, and mythic quests.

title: The Mabinogion
description: A collection of medieval Welsh tales containing Celtic mythological motifs, heroic legends, magical transformations, and Arthurian material.

title: The Nibelungenlied
description: A medieval German heroic epic involving Siegfried, Kriemhild, dragons, treasure, betrayal, and mythic heroic tradition.

title: West African Folk-Tales
description: A collection of West African folktales, including trickster and animal stories often associated with Anansi and oral storytelling traditions.

title: Australian Legendary Tales
description: A collection of Aboriginal Australian legends and Dreaming-related stories recorded from oral traditions.

title: Te Tohunga
description: A collection of ancient legends and traditions of the Maori, including mythic figures, ancestral stories, ritual specialists, and sacred knowledge.

title: Legends of Ma-ui a Demi God of Polynesia
description: A collection of Polynesian legends about Maui, the trickster and culture hero known for feats such as fishing up islands and slowing the sun.

title: The Popol Vuh
description: The mythic and heroic sagas of the K'iche' Maya, including creation narratives, the Hero Twins, divine ancestry, and sacred history.

title: Myths of the Australian Aborigines
description: A collection of myths about the Dreamtime and traditional beliefs of the indigenous peoples of Australia.

title: The Odyssey
description: An ancient Greek epic poem attributed to Homer, recounting the long, mythic journey of the hero Odysseus returning to Ithaca after the fall of Troy.

title: The Bhagavad Gita
description: A central sacred scripture of Hinduism, presenting a philosophical dialogue between Prince Arjuna and the god Krishna on duty, honor, and the nature of reality.

title: Beowulf
description: The oldest surviving epic poem in Old English, telling the story of a Scandinavian hero who battles the monster Grendel, his vengeful mother, and a dragon.

title: The Aeneid
description: An ancient Roman epic by Virgil that follows Aeneas, a Trojan hero who travels to Italy after the fall of Troy and becomes the ancestor of the Romans.

title: The Code of Hammurabi
description: One of the oldest deciphered writings of significant length in the world, providing a comprehensive Babylonian law code from ancient Mesopotamia.

title: The Mahabharata
description: A massive ancient Indian epic that narrates the Kurukshetra War between the Pandava and Kaurava princes, alongside vast philosophical and religious treatises.
